page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across multiple domains. The system achieves average multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside average results in software compilation and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 946.87 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 162.96 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-threaded tasks show some limitations, with weak performance in Geekbench single-core, text processing, and single-threaded gzip compression. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier, processing 20.4 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and Nitro hypervisor deliver a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-speed local NVMe storage without the overhead of traditional x86 licensing. This makes the instance highly resource-dense for memory-bound workloads. The primary tradeoff lies in single-threaded execution speeds and cryptographic performance, which rank in the lower percentiles. Consequently, this instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications that benefit from local scratch space, such as medium-sized databases, caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.4xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers 5 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU and database performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 20.4 million operations per second, though single-threaded tasks and cryptographic operations exhibit weaker performance. This resource-dense profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suited for database hosting, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing workloads requiring local scratch storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
